pending.body: [src/cli/project.rs:444-478,617-668] `execute_create_from_bead` only preflights active `task-runs.ndjson` entries. A bead-backed project that already exists but has never started has no lineage row, so a second `project create-from-bead --project-id ...` for the same bead slips past the new guard and falls into `claim_bead_in_br`. That path returns a generic br-claim failure and transitions the milestone controller to `needs_operator`, even though this is just the duplicate-not-started case the bead policy is supposed to reject cleanly. The command should detect an existing same-bead project before the br claim and return a targeted duplicate-bead error naming that project instead of taking the operator-recovery path.

[src/adapters/fs.rs:4651-4659,5147-5191] `record_task_run_start` still rewrites every non-terminal `run_id: None` lineage row to `failed` before it evaluates same-bead conflicts. In an upgraded workspace with a legacy active row, `run start` on a second project for the same bead will silently auto-fail the old active entry and proceed, instead of surfacing the repair guidance that `project create-from-bead` now emits for the same corrupted/orphaned state. That leaves create/start semantics misaligned and still lets corrupted same-bead lineage be bypassed rather than rejected.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 8 -> Plan and Implement
